channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Un paseo por los ‘pubs’ de Dublín, pero sin alcohol
item.description: Un recorrido por algunos de los bares más famosos de la capital irlandesa revela una nueva filosofía: no necesitas alcohol para divertirte.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia and Paulo Nunes Dos Santos

media:content.media:credit: Paulo Nunes dos Santos for The New York Times

item.title: ¿Timothée Chalamet podrá romper la maldición de los Oscar?
item.description: El Oscar al mejor actor casi nunca es para hombres jóvenes, aunque este actor de 30 años tiene su mejor oportunidad hasta ahora con ‘Marty Supremo’.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Buchanan

item.title: New Jersey Is Reconsidering Legalizing Casinos Outside Atlantic City
item.description: Legislation has been introduced to authorize two new casinos at the Meadowlands and Monmouth Park racetracks to compete against the three new casinos planned for New York City.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rubinstein and Tracey Tully

media:content.media:credit: Jonah Markowitz for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Casinos in Atlantic City, N.J.

item.title: The Challenge of Helping Homeless People Who Avoid New York’s Shelters
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has been reluctant to force people indoors, even in dire weather. But conditions, whether on the streets or in shelters, can be dangerous.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg and Elizabeth A. Harris
item.category: New York City Health and Hospitals Corp

media:content.media:description: A team from NYC Health + Hospitals, the public health system, work with homeless people in Manhattan as part of a program called WARM, which helps people who sleep outdoors.

item.title: Picture Prompt | Gas Pump
item.description: What do you think this image is communicating?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

media:content.media:description: The conflict in Iran has disrupted the world’s supply of oil and driven up prices at the pump.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 13, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Late Night Unwraps ‘War’ vs. ‘Excursion’ vs. ‘Both’
item.description: The “Late Night” host Seth Meyers said President Trump is letting Americans “choose your own adventure” in the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Qué significa malevolent
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 85 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Oscar 2026: ¿quién ganará mejor película, actor y actriz?
item.description: ‘Pecadores’ y ‘Una batalla tras otra’ han estado codo con codo toda la temporada. Esto es lo que nuestro experto cree que ganará.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Buchanan

item.title: Word of the Day: malevolent
item.description: This word has appeared in 85 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Indian Kitchens Face Fuel Shortage From War in Middle East
item.description: The fuel that powers Indian kitchens has been harder to get since the start of the war, which effectively shut a critical shipping lane for gas imports that India’s population relies on.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:description: Workers cooked food using firewood instead of gas outside a restaurant in Bengaluru, India, amid disruptions in the liquid petroleum gas supply.

item.title: La crisis del petróleo asesta otro golpe a la economía mundial
item.description: Los países ya afectados por la ruptura del orden comercial internacional, la guerra en Ucrania y el caos de los legisladores estadounidenses se enfrentan a daños económicos potencialmente duraderos.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Cohen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Petroleros y cargueros el miércoles en el estrecho de Ormuz

item.title: Europe Is in Danger, and It’s Doing Basically Nothing About It
item.description: European politicians have colluded in their dependency on a rogue America.
item.dc:creator: Anton Jäger

item.title: China’s Plan to Spur Consumer Spending Is a Mirage
item.description: China may never be able to realize its longtime promise to shift away from an overreliance on exports.
item.dc:creator: Anne Stevenson-Yang

item.title: A Visit to a Temple at the Heart of the Thailand-Cambodia Conflict
item.description: A rare visit to a Khmer temple on Thailand and Cambodia’s border showed how deadly clashes between the two countries have scarred a heritage site.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee and Lauren DeCicca

media:content.media:credit: Lauren DeCicca for The New York Times

item.title: Bad News for Friggatriskaidekaphobics: 2026 Has Three Fridays the 13th
item.description: It’s the first time since 2015 that the combination of the day and date associated with bad luck has recurred three times in a calendar year.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb

media:content.media:description: The Gregorian calendar runs in 400-year cycles, offering 59 years in which the 13th falls on a Friday three times. 2026 is one of them.

item.title: Honda Scraps Plans for E.V.s While Start-Ups Forge Ahead
item.description: The Japanese company’s retreat echoes moves by other traditional carmakers as the industry divides between electric vehicle haves and have-nots.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Honda once planned to retool its plant in Marysville, Ohio, to make electric vehicles. The company announced this week that it was scrapping plans for three battery-powered models.

item.title: Corrections: March 13, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Friday, March 13, 2026.

item.title: How the War in Iran Could Help China and Change Asia
item.description: American officials have said for years that they would prioritize the Indo-Pacific. Now they’re moving warships, missiles and air defenses out for a war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Damien Cave, Choe Sang-Hun, Javier C. Hernández and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

media:content.media:description: Fighter jets on the aircraft carrier U.S.S. Abraham Lincoln in 2024. A carrier group including the ship was redirected from the South China Sea to the Middle East in January.

item.title: How the Iran War Threatens the Global Food, Energy and Other Supplies
item.description: Beyond its effects on oil and gas, the unfolding war in the Middle East is roiling shipping and airfreight, threatening the availability of a vast range of goods.
item.dc:creator: Peter S. Goodman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The container terminal in Newark.

item.title: What to Know About the U.S. Lasers Being Used to Counter Iranian Attacks
item.description: High-energy lasers can burn drones in the sky like a blowtorch. But they can be stymied by weather, and making them at scale will be a challenge.
item.dc:creator: Farah Stockman
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Nic Antaya for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A high-energy laser on display during a manufacturing conference in Detroit last year.

item.title: Yes, He Actually Tried to Woo Me by Drone
item.description: My Nigerian tech bro considered flowers to be “too analog.”
item.dc:creator: Solape Adetutu Adeyemi

item.title: Surging Energy Costs Put German Industry ‘Really in Danger’
item.description: Four years after the Russian invasion of Ukraine sent energy prices soaring, the war in Iran is posing another challenge to efforts to revive European factories.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson and Andreas Meichsner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Carbon Caps and Emissions Trading Programs

item.title: It Took a Village (and a Parents’ Group and a Knitting Group) to Get Married
item.description: A blizzard forced Priscilla Villalobos and Timothy Kolk to rethink their New York wedding, and find a new officiant — fast.
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan

item.title: When It Came to Their Vows, Rapper Remy Banks and Ashley Condina Didn’t Miss a Beat
item.description: At their Valentine’s Day wedding, Mr. Banks read the verses of a song he wrote for his girlfriend of about six years, the artist Ashley Condina.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: The Amtrak Ticket That Kept Getting Extended
item.description: Richard Hudock and Justin Goldman talked for about eight hours the night they met at a rooftop party. They have barely stopped since.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: He Loves Crosswords. She Wrote the Book on Them.
item.description: Adrienne Raphel and David Rosenberg had little luck in dating — until a riff about animals and Italian food brought them together.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: Quote of the Day: Pints Still Flow Freely in Dublin, but More Are Alcohol-Free
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, March 13, 2026.

item.title: Canada Plans Military Expansion in Arctic, Following Trump Threats
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney said Canada would build three bases in the region. The government also plans to improve infrastructure and airports in the north.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen

media:content.media:description: Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney, visited the Royal Canadian Air Force 440 Transport Squadron, in Yellowknife, Northwest Territories, on Thursday.

item.title: Daniel Radcliffe Shines in ‘Every Brilliant Thing’ on Broadway
item.description: The actor’s fondness for the audience radiates outward in this delightful interactive play about naming and noticing the good in the world.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw

media:content.media:description: Daniel Radcliffe, center right, with audience members in “Every Brilliant Thing” at the Hudson Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: Suspect in Michigan Synagogue Attack Worked at Popular Restaurant
item.description: The authorities identified the suspect as a naturalized U.S. citizen. Residents of Dearborn Heights, Mich., said he took orders at the business.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman, Ryan Patrick Hooper and Sheelagh McNeill

media:content.media:credit: Sarah Rice for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A heavy police presence in West Bloomfield Township, Mich., after the attack.

item.title: Trump no está preparado para lo que inició en Cuba
item.description: Cualquier resolución que se forje en el actual enfrentamiento entre Washington y La Habana corre el riesgo de ser una victoria vacía.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Sabatini and Katrin Hansing

item.title: ‘The Pitt’ Season 2, Episode 10 Recap: Invisible String
item.description: Family ties bind the characters together in this week’s episode, in ways both beautiful and suffocating.
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins

media:content.media:description: Dr. Mohan (Supriya Ganesh) had a panic attack, brought on partly by the incessant calling of her mother.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 13, 2026
item.description: James McCarron and Rachel Souza open our solving weekend.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

item.title: Oil Prices Barely Budge After Trump Lifts Russia Sanctions
item.description: After surging about 10 percent on Thursday, oil prices had little reaction to the decision by President Trump to waive sanctions on the sale of some Russian crude.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump Removes Sanctions on Russia to Help Oil Flow Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said it was “unfortunate” that the move could benefit Russia, but maintained that it was only for the short term.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Thursday that Russia would not benefit significantly from the sanctions relief but acknowledged that it would see some financial benefit.

item.title: Cuba liberará a 51 presos en medio de presiones de EE. UU.
item.description: El gobierno de Trump ha estado intentando asfixiar al gobierno cubano mediante un bloqueo petrolero. La liberación de prisioneros parece ser un esfuerzo por apaciguar a Washington.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas

media:content.media:description: El edificio del Capitolio en La Habana.

item.title: Andrea Constand Testifies in Support of Another Bill Cosby Accuser
item.description: Lawyers for a woman who has sued Mr. Cosby, accusing him of sexual assault, called Ms. Constand as a witness in the case, which is now being tried in California.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens and Graham Bowley

media:content.media:description: Andrea Constand, whose testimony led to Bill Cosby’s conviction that was later overturned, testified in a civil case brought by another Cosby accuser.

item.title: ‘Antigone (This Play I Read in High School)’ Review: Tragically Uneven
item.description: Anna Ziegler’s feminist take on Sophocles tries to tie in reproductive politics, but the play keeps trampling over its own ideas.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw
item.category: Antigone (This Play I Read in High School) (Play)

media:content.media:description: Susannah Perkins, left, and Celia Keenan-Bolger in “Antigone (This Play I Read in High School)” at the Public Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: Ernie Anastos, New York’s Everyman Newscaster, Dies at 82
item.description: A television journalist for four decades with 30 Emmy Awards, Mr. Anastos especially enjoyed delivering good news.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin

media:content.media:description: Ernie Anastos on the WNYW Fox set in 2010.

item.title: Cuba Pledges to Release 51 Prisoners Amid U.S. Pressure
item.description: The Trump administration has been trying to choke the Cuban government through an oil blockade. The prisoner release appears to be an effort to appease Washington.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas, Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and Frances Robles

media:content.media:description: The Capitol building in Havana.

item.title: F.B.I. Investigating Synagogue Attack as ‘Targeted Act of Violence’
item.description: A driver rammed a vehicle into a synagogue outside Detroit on Thursday. Authorities are investigating the attacker’s motive.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: En Chile, la toma de mando de José Antonio Kast aviva la euforia conservadora
item.description: La llegada al poder del nuevo presidente es el hito más reciente en una tendencia más amplia de conservadurismo y alineamiento con Trump en Latinoamérica.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola

media:content.media:description: El presidente de Chile, José Antonio Kast, y su esposa, María Pía Adriasola, en el palacio presidencial de La Moneda, en Santiago de Chile, el miércoles.

item.title: University Shooting Being Investigated as an Act of Terrorism, Officials Say
item.description: The authorities are investigating the shooting at Old Dominion University in Norfolk, Va., as an act of terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling

media:content.media:credit: Kendall Warner/The Virginian-Pilot, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: The police responded to a report of a shooting on the campus of Old Dominion University in Norfolk, Va., on Thursday morning.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Thursday
item.description: Iran’s new supreme leader delivered a forceful message in his first public statement since succeeding his slain father, as the Israeli military bombarded Tehran and the Lebanese capital with strikes.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Beirut residents watching the aftermath of an Israeli airstrike in the Lebanese capital on Thursday.

item.title: What We Know About Michigan Synagogue Attack on Temple Israel
item.description: The attacker who drove into Temple Israel in West Bloomfield Township, a 41-year-old naturalized U.S. citizen, is dead. No one else was killed.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri

media:content.media:credit: Sarah Rice for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Emergency personnel after an attack at Temple Israel in West Bloomfield Township, Mich., on Thursday.

item.title: U.S. KC-135 Refueling Plane Crashes in Iraq, Military Says
item.description: The crash was not because of hostile fire or friendly fire, U.S. Central Command said.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Greg Jaffe and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A U.S. KC-135 aircraft flying over Tel Aviv last week.

item.title: War in Iran Has India Scrambling to Keep Stoves Lit for Cooking
item.description: India relies on huge quantities of cooking gas that is normally shipped from the Persian Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Pragati K.B. and Alex Travelli
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Workers cooked food using firewood instead of gas outside a restaurant in Bengaluru, India, amid disruptions in the liquid petroleum gas supply.

item.title: Meta Delays Rollout of New A.I. Model After Performance Concerns
item.description: The tech giant pushed back the timeline after spending billions to be on the cutting edge of artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Eli Tan

media:content.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The new A.I. model that Meta’s chief executive, Mark Zuckerberg, is pushing had been targeted for release this month.

item.title: Iran War Maps: Tracking the Mideast Conflict
item.description: Maps show where U.S. and Israel have struck Iran, and where Iran has retaliated.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
